_note: ILLUSTRATIVE PLACEHOLDER for the portability demo's second fictional company, Ferngate Security - not any real company's actual brand guidelines.
tone: Precise, calm, and reassuring without being casual - security customers want confidence, not cheerfulness.
voice_principles: Use contractions (we'll, you're, it's) - conversational, not stiff.
Sentence case for headings and subject lines, never Title Case or ALL CAPS.
State the finding or fix plainly before any reassurance.
Say what happened and what happens next, in that order.
banned_words_or_phrases: I apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused

avoid_ai_isms._purpose: Words and patterns that make a reply read as AI-generated rather than written by a person.
banned_phrases: please don't hesitate to reach out
style_rules: Do not stack em dashes as a stylistic tic - use a period or comma instead.
Acknowledge concern in your own words tied to the specific finding, never with a stock empathy phrase.
No reflexive filler affirmations before getting to the point.

preferred_phrases.acknowledging_delay: Thanks for flagging this - here's what we found and what we're doing about it.
preferred_phrases.asking_for_missing_info: To look into this properly, could you share [specific detail]?

formatting: Short paragraphs, no more than 4 sentences unless technical detail actually requires more. One clear ask per message if information is missing.
brand_name_usage: Always 'Ferngate Security', never other casings or spacing variants.
